title: Tark Shaastra — 24 Hours Hackathon
description: A flagship event where innovators collaborate to build impactful solutions within 24 hours. Participants work in teams to transform ideas into prototypes, tackling real-world challenges through creativity, coding, and problem-solving. Judged on technical implementation, innovation, impact, UI/UX, and presentation quality.

title: Drone Grand Prix
description: A competitive event where teams bring their pre-built drones to complete a specially designed obstacle course. The challenge evaluates speed, stability, control, and durability while navigating multiple task zones. Participants must operate their drones with precision to finish the track in the least possible time.

title: Structural Survival
description: A hands-on engineering challenge where teams must design and construct a bridge model using simple materials, which will be tested with increasing loads until failure. The event evaluates structural ingenuity, material efficiency, and load-bearing capacity.

title: Aero-Struct Challenges
description: A two-round engineering design showdown where teams first build a load-bearing chassis from popsicle sticks and cardboard, then craft an aerodynamic body tested under live airflow from an adjustable-speed fan. Judging focuses on structural strength, material optimization, and aerodynamic performance.

title: Short Film Generation (Red Cross)
description: Blood donation is a vital humanitarian act that saves millions of lives every year. Despite its importance, voluntary blood donation among youth requires continuous awareness and motivation. Recognizing the power of creative media in influencing social behavior, Lakshya 2.0 in collaboration with the Indian Red Cross Society, Ahmedabad District Branch & Youth Red Cross LDCE, proposes to organize a Nation-wide Advertisement Making Competition focused on Blood Donation Awareness. This initiative aims to engage school, college and university students as change-makers, encouraging them to creatively communicate the importance of blood donation through impactful video advertisements.

title: Filter Fusion
description: This sustainability-driven technical event challenges participants to design and develop functional filtration systems using discarded and recyclable materials. Rooted in circular economy principles, teams create innovative solutions for water, air, or wastewater filtration while focusing on efficiency, affordability, and environmental responsibility. The competition promotes hands-on environmental problem-solving and green engineering practices. By transforming waste into valuable filtration systems, the event inspires future engineers to integrate sustainability with innovation.

title: Rapid CAD Arena
description: A high-intensity CAD design challenge where participants convert engineering requirements into precise digital models under strict time limits. Competitors create 2D drawings and 3D models while maintaining accuracy and speed. The event rewards those who combine technical precision with rapid execution.

title: Circuit Debugging
description: A two-round electronics challenge where teams first simulate and debug circuits digitally, then physically build and verify the same circuit on a breadboard. Performance is judged on troubleshooting ability, accuracy, speed, and conceptual clarity.

title: RoboMaze (Maze Solver)
description: RoboMaze is an electrifying robotics challenge where teams design and program autonomous robots to navigate through a complex maze in the shortest possible time. The event tests algorithm efficiency, sensor integration, precision control, and real-time decision-making under competitive conditions. Blending innovation, coding, electronics, and mechanical design, RoboMaze provides a powerful platform to showcase intelligent automation and engineering excellence.

title: C2C Display Presentation
description: C2C 2026 is a dynamic technical conclave designed to bridge the journey from innovative concepts to real-world creation. The event brings together aspiring engineers to present ideas, showcase projects, and engage in insightful technical discussions that emphasize practical application and sustainable development. Focused on research, innovation, and interdisciplinary collaboration, the symposium provides a platform for students to demonstrate analytical thinking, problem-solving skills, and forward-looking engineering solutions.

title: PromptCraft
description: PromptCraft is a three-level artificial intelligence competition designed to test precision, creativity, and strategic thinking in prompt engineering. Participants craft optimized prompts to generate accurate visuals and Al-powered outputs, progressing from simple recreations to complex creative challenges. The grand finale requires teams to develop a thematic Al-generated reel, judged on storytelling and innovation. Bridging communication and technology, PromptCraft celebrates mastery in interacting effectively with generative Al systems.

title: Imposter Coding Quest
description: Imposter Coding Quest is a strategic coding competition that blends technical expertise with observation and tactical thinking. In teams of four, one participant is secretly assigned the role of an impostor who subtly sabotages the coding process while others attempt to solve the problem correctly. After individual coding rounds, teams must identify the impostor based on logic quality and behavior. Combining programming skills with psychological strategy, this event offers a unique and engaging twist to conventional coding contests.

title: TechTrace
description: TechTrace is a dynamic campus-wide technical treasure hunt that challenges participants to apply logical reasoning, coding knowledge, and problem-solving skills in a fast-paced team competition. Through a series of tech-themed clues, programming puzzles, and interactive checkpoints, teams must decode, strategize, and race against time to uncover the final treasure. Blending concepts like algorithms, binary logic, and analytical thinking with teamwork and coordination, TechTrace offers a fun yet intellectually stimulating experience for engineering students across all branches. [EXACTLY 2 MEMBERS COMPULSORY]
organizer: GDG On Campus LDCE & MSA LDCE

title: SpaceTalks Live
description: SpaceTalks Live is an interactive podcast-style session featuring distinguished industry professionals and technical experts in conversation with student moderators. The discussion explores real-world applications of technology, emerging industry trends, and practical career guidance. With live audience interaction and a dedicated Q&A segment, the event bridges academic learning with professional insights. Designed to inspire and inform, SpaceTalks Live fosters awareness, communication skills, and industry readiness.

title: Balance Track
description: Balance Track is an advanced robotics competition focused on designing a self-balancing autonomous robot capable of navigating a complex track while carrying a mounted payload. Unlike conventional line-following contests, the challenge integrates dynamic stability, precise control algorithms, and real-time sensor feedback. Participants must minimize oscillations and water spillage while maintaining accuracy and speed across sharp turns and variable track conditions. The event offers hands-on exposure to control systems, embedded programming, and robotics engineering under real-world constraints.

title: LineXplorer
description: Teams design and build autonomous robots to follow complex line tracks at maximum speed. Tests embedded programming, sensor integration, and mechanical design for fast and accurate line detection.

title: Engineer of the Year
description: Engineer of the Year is the flagship multi-stage competition designed to identify the most versatile and capable engineering mind. Open to both internal and external participants, the event evaluates aptitude, general awareness, technical proficiency, and real-time problem-solving through progressive elimination rounds. Finalists defend their solutions before a judging panel, demonstrating analytical clarity and confidence under pressure. The competition celebrates all-round excellence and crowns the ultimate “Last Engineer Standing.”

title: Market Masters
description:
Market Masters is an insightful seminar designed to equip participants with the knowledge and strategies needed to invest wisely and build long-term wealth. The session focuses on understanding the fundamentals of the stock market, exploring diverse investment avenues, and developing a disciplined approach to financial growth.

Participants will learn how to analyze opportunities, manage risk effectively, and avoid common pitfalls that often lead to financial losses. The seminar emphasizes practical, real-world insights—covering topics such as portfolio diversification, long-term investing principles, and smart decision-making in uncertain market conditions.

By combining financial education with strategic thinking, Market Masters aims to empower individuals to make informed investment choices, safeguard their capital, and steadily grow their wealth over time.

title: The Painted Realm
description: The Painted Realm is a creative showcase inviting participants to express their imagination of the universe through live artistic expression. During the on-spot Art Jam, students create artwork inspired by a surprise theme, representing galaxies, astronauts, celestial phenomena, and futuristic space ideas. Completed works are exhibited as part of a mini art gallery during the fest. The event beautifully integrates creativity with curiosity about space science, encouraging interdisciplinary participation.

title: ChemVista
description: ChemVista is a creative technical competition where participants visually communicate powerful engineering and sustainability themes through impactful posters. The event encourages artistic expression combined with technical awareness, covering topics such as green technology, innovation, and social responsibility. Judged on creativity, clarity, and relevance, ChemVista promotes thoughtful communication of engineering ideas. It provides a vibrant platform for students to blend imagination with technical insight.

title: RoboRush
description: Engineer FPV-enabled robots and guide them through an intense obstacle course filled with ramps, tunnels, sharp turns, and barriers. Participants operate their robots entirely through a live first-person camera feed without direct line of sight.

title: Renewable ReMakers
description: A hands-on sustainability challenge where teams design working prototypes that transform waste materials into useful products . The event evaluates technical feasibility, environmental impact, and innovation in green engineering.

title: Auction & Creation
description: An electronics innovation challenge where Teams earn points through a quiz, bid for components in an auction, and build their own battery-powered car under time constraints. The challenge ends with an exciting drag race, where performance, speed, and smart design decide the winner.

title: SimulChem
description: SimulChem is a technical design and simulation challenge where teams develop and optimize a complete process flowsheet using industry-grade simulation software. Participants strategically select equipment within a fixed virtual budget while achieving product purity and performance targets. The competition emphasizes analytical thinking, economic reasoning, and process optimization skills. SimulChem bridges classroom theory with real-world industrial design challenges in chemical engineering.

title: Chem-CarBurn
description: Chem-CarBurn is an exciting model car competition where teams design and build reaction-powered miniature vehicles using engineering fundamentals. Competing on a predefined track, cars are judged on speed, stability, innovation, and propulsion efficiency. The event promotes creativity, teamwork, and hands-on problem-solving in a dynamic racing environment. It transforms core engineering principles into an engaging and competitive experience.

title: ChemQuest
description: ChemQuest is a multi-round technical quiz designed to test core chemical engineering knowledge and quick-thinking ability. Covering subjects such as thermodynamics, reaction engineering, and process equipment, the event blends direct questioning, rapid-fire rounds, and case-study challenges. It encourages conceptual clarity, analytical reasoning, and healthy academic competition. ChemQuest delivers an intellectually stimulating and interactive learning experience.

title: Eco-Clean Water Fest
description: Eco-Clean Water Fest is a hands-on sustainability challenge focused on designing adsorption columns using agro-bio waste materials for industrial wastewater treatment. Participants demonstrate eco-friendly and cost-effective solutions capable of achieving significant dye removal efficiency. The event promotes circular economy principles and environmental engineering innovation. By combining experimentation with sustainability, it inspires practical solutions for cleaner water and greener industries.

title: The Changemaker
description: The Changemaker is an interactive live podcast session exploring education, social impact, and youth participation in nation-building. Featuring insights from grassroots social initiatives, the session offers students a realistic understanding of how NGOs function and create measurable impact. Participants gain exposure to leadership journeys, career lessons, and meaningful avenues for volunteering. Designed to inspire responsibility beyond academics, the event fosters awareness, empathy, and purpose-driven thinking.

title: Quiz Crete
description: The Civil Engineering Technical Quiz is a multi-round competitive event designed to test participants’ conceptual clarity, practical exposure, and analytical thinking in core civil engineering domains. Beginning with a written elimination round, the competition progresses through visual identification of real-world engineering scenarios. The final rapid-fire and buzzer round evaluates speed, accuracy, and confidence under pressure, making it an intellectually stimulating platform for aspiring civil engineers.

title: Youth Parliament
description: LDCE Youth Parliament 2026 is a dynamic platform that brings together young minds to engage in meaningful discussion on national, social and policy-related issues. It aims to simulate the functioning of a parliamentary system, allowing participants to understand how ideas are presented, debated and transformed into decisions within a democratic framework.
organizer: Leo Club of Ahmedabad Main LDCE

title: Pitch the Idea
description: Teams pitch their startup ideas to a panel of judges. Presentations are evaluated on innovation, feasibility, market potential, and delivery.

title: ByteHunt
description: A tech-based competitive hunt where participants tackle a series of byte-sized technical challenges spanning coding, logic, and digital problem-solving. Teams race through clues and tasks that test their breadth of knowledge across computing concepts.

title: VR Game Zone [Tech]
description: Step into the future with Meta Quest VR headsets and experience immersive virtual reality gaming like never before. With 10 active VR stations running free and premium games from the Meta Quest Store, this is your chance to explore the world of Extended Reality (XR) firsthand. A hands-on technology exposure zone powered by Khodiyar CAD Center, designed to give students a taste of next-generation Industry 4.0 / 5.0 tools through pure experiential learning.
organizer: Khodiyar CAD Center & Khodiyar Technovation Pvt. Ltd.

title: Digital Twin Workshop
description: A two-day hands-on workshop introducing students to the cutting-edge world of Digital Twin technology — where 3D models, IoT data, and simulation platforms converge. Day 1 covers the conceptual foundations: Digital Twin architecture, IT & OT integration, and real-world case studies in smart cities, infrastructure, and manufacturing using platforms like Autodesk Tandem and Unity Industry. Day 2 dives into practical demonstration — participants build a basic Digital Twin demo, integrate model data, and explore visualization workflows. Conducted by Khodiyar Technovation Pvt. Ltd., this workshop opens career pathways in BIM, Simulation, XR, Smart Infrastructure, and Digital Engineering.
organizer: Khodiyar CAD Center & Khodiyar Technovation Pvt. Ltd.

title: Civil Chaos [Civil]
description: Civil Chaos – Meme Competition is a creative platform for civil engineering students to express real-world engineering issues through humor and satire while maintaining technical relevance. Participants are required to design memes that clearly highlight an engineering fault along with its potential outcomes or impacts. Beyond just identifying problems, each entry must also include practicable and realistic corrective measures. (Refer to rulebook for thematic focus)
importantNote: LAST DATE FOR ONLINE MEME SUBMISSION: 16th APRIL
